channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: KMTC Allows Deferred Admission for Students Who Missed March 2026 Intake
item.description: Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) has opened its admissions portal to allow students who missed the March 2026 intake to defer and join the September 2026 class. This initiative offers learners a ...

item.title: A Glimpse of Venice in 1954 Through Vintage Photography
item.description: In 1954, Venice retained its status as one of the most captivating cities in the world. Largely untouched by modern development, the city exuded a serene and graceful charm, with gondolas gliding thro...

item.title: Government institutions’ response during national emergency detailed in inquiry report
item.description: Recently, an inquiry report has come out showing how different government institutions in Tanzania responded during the national emergency. The report highlights the roles of various state bodies and ...

item.title: Minister says more students now prefer Nigerian universities over studying abroad
item.description: The Minister of Education, Tunji Alausa, don calm people wey dey worry say plenty Nigerian students dey run go foreign universities. For one interview with Channels Television, he yan say the trend do...

item.title: Allegations of Corruption Shake Romania’s Top Judicial System
item.description: Romania is facing a deepening crisis in its justice system following a documentary exposing alleged corruption among senior magistrates and politicians. The film suggested that administrative manoeuvr...

item.title: David Venturella, former private prison executive, appointed as ICE director
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has appointed David Venturella, a former executive at the private prison company Geo Group, as the new director of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (I...

item.title: Life and Culture in France During the Mid-1950s
item.description: The article offers a vivid glimpse into life in France during the mid-1950s, a period known as the Trente Glorieuses or 'Thirty Glorious Years.' Post-war France experienced a mix of modernization and ...

item.title: JAMB Lowers Cut-Off Marks Amid Concerns Over Education Standards in Nigeria
item.description: For years, Nigeria dey maintain say 200 na the minimum score wey person suppose score for JAMB to show say dem fit handle tertiary education. But as of 2026, the minimum cut-off don drop reach 150, an...

item.title: Highlights and Honours from the 2026 AMVCAs Night
item.description: Di 2026 Africa Magic Viewers’ Choice Awards (AMVCAs) no just bring glitz and glamour, e show how African film industry dey grow steadily. Dis year, di focus na to celebrate di journey of people wey do...

item.title: Met Police live facial recognition trial leads to arrest of long-term wanted woman in London
item.description: The Metropolitan Police in London have reported a notable success in their live facial recognition trial, following the arrest of a woman who had been wanted for over two decades. The operation, part ...

item.title: Christiane Amanpour Expresses Concerns Over Potential Changes at CNN Under David Ellison Ownership
item.description: Veteran CNN journalist Christiane Amanpour expressed concerns about the future of her network during a journalism summit in London. Speaking at the Truth Tellers Sir Harry Evans Investigative Journali...

item.title: A Look at Stanley Kubrick’s Early Film ‘Killer’s Kiss’ Through 30 Iconic Photos
item.description: Stanley Kubrick’s 1955 film 'Killer’s Kiss' offers an early glimpse into the legendary director’s visual style and filmmaking ingenuity. This American film noir, lasting only 67 minutes, was Kubrick’s...

item.title: Pentagon deploys Anthropic AI to address cyber vulnerabilities despite plans to end partnership
item.description: The Pentagon has started using Anthropic’s AI model called Mythos to identify and patch software vulnerabilities across various government systems. The initiative is part of Anthropic’s 'Project Glass...

item.title: JPMorgan CEO warns Labour over leftward shift amid UK leadership tensions
item.description: Jamie Dimon, the chairman and CEO of JPMorgan Chase, has voiced concerns over the Labour Party’s current leadership struggles under Sir Keir Starmer. Speaking to Bloomberg TV, Dimon praised Starmer’s ...

item.title: Al Nassr Draw With Al Hilal As Ronaldo Awaits First Saudi Pro League Title
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo still dey wait to lift him first Saudi Pro League trophy after Al Nassr draw 1-1 with Al Hilal on Tuesday. The draw happen because goalkeeper Bento make mistake for injury time, thro...

item.title: Father leaves corporate career to be more present for family after emotional wake-up call
item.description: Terry Murton spent years chasing success in the corporate world, believing that climbing the ladder would bring him happiness and respect. Despite achieving a management position in mining, the cost w...

item.title: Hints and Solutions for Today’s New York Times ‘Connections’ Puzzle
item.description: The New York Times’ online game 'Connections' is known for challenging players to group 16 words into four categories based on shared themes. While some groupings are straightforward, others can be mo...

item.title: Key lessons for a successful roll-out of South Africa’s new HIV prevention injection
item.description: South Africa is preparing to roll out lenacapavir (LEN), a twice-a-year injection offering potent HIV prevention, particularly for teenage girls and young women aged 16–25. Despite its effectiveness i...

item.title: Finance Minister Briefs IMF on Budget Plans and Economic Reforms
item.description: Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb met with the visiting IMF mission in Islamabad to discuss preparations for Pakistan’s upcoming federal budget and broader economic reforms. During the meeting, he b...

item.title: Reflections on History and the Tango 'Cambalache'
item.description: In this blog post dated May 12, 2026, Juan Manuel Grijalvo reflects on history, human behavior, and the famous tango 'Cambalache.' The author discusses popular sayings such as 'I forgive but I do not ...
